auth.companySlugHint: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
auth.name: Name

import.instructionsBody: Use the downloadable template: .xlsx only, max file size 1 MB, column keys on row 1, hints on row 2, and your data from row 3. Up to 200 data rows per file.
import.instructionsStep1Body: Column keys (headers). Do not rename, delete, or reorder columns.
import.instructionsStep2Body: Short hint per column from the template—read-only reference.
import.instructionsStep3Body: Your candidate rows. Empty rows are ignored.
import.instructionsFooterHint: Tip: download the template first, fill it in Excel, then upload here. Image columns must be HTTPS links to JPEG, PNG, GIF, or WebP; the server downloads and stores them securely. In preview you can still upload images with the uploader.
import.instructionDates: Dates must be YYYY-MM-DD (calendar date, stored in UTC).
import.instructionPassportUrl: Passport image is required: HTTPS URL to an image, or an existing file UUID from your company. You can upload from the preview step if needed.
import.instructionStatus: Column status must be DRAFT or UNDER_PROCEDURE (empty defaults to UNDER_PROCEDURE).

columnRules.full_name_ar: Arabic full name. Required when status is UNDER_PROCEDURE (published row).
columnRules.full_name_en: English full name. Required when status is UNDER_PROCEDURE.
columnRules.nationality: Nationality code (e.g. SA). Required for UNDER_PROCEDURE.
columnRules.passport_no: Passport number; unique within your company.
columnRules.status: DRAFT or UNDER_PROCEDURE. Empty cell defaults to UNDER_PROCEDURE.
columnRules.passport_image_url: Required. HTTPS URL to an image (JPEG/PNG/GIF/WebP), or an existing file UUID. Images are downloaded and stored.
columnRules.visa_image_url: Optional. Same HTTPS image rules if filled.
columnRules.flight_ticket_image_url: Optional. Same HTTPS image rules if filled.
columnRules.personal_picture_url: Optional. Same HTTPS image rules if filled.

import.validateFailed: Could not validate the file. Check the format and try again.
import.previewAllValid: All {count} row(s) are valid and ready to import.
import.previewMixed: {valid} row(s) can be imported; {invalid} row(s) have errors and will be skipped.
import.confirmBody: You are about to import {count} candidate(s). Rows with errors ({skip}) will not be imported.
import.resultChunk: Chunk size used: {size} rows per batch

errors.HR_RECRUITMENT_IMPORT_013: File does not belong to your company
errors.HR_RECRUITMENT_IMPORT_015: Passport already exists in the system
errors.HR_RECRUITMENT_IMPORT_018: Draft must have at least one field filled
errors.HR_RECRUITMENT_IMPORT_022: Could not download image from URL
errors.HR_RECRUITMENT_IMPORT_023: URL did not return a valid image (JPEG, PNG, GIF, or WebP)

import.fileTooLargeImport: File must be 1 MB or smaller (.xlsx).

employment.minimumSalaryError: Salary must be at least the company minimum
employment.deductionMethodNextMonthWarning: A new deduction method has been saved and will be implemented starting next month.
employment.tooltipOperatingPlatform: The delivery platform this employee operates on (Jahez, Hungerstation, etc.)
employment.tooltipPlatformUserNo: The employee's unique ID on the selected platform
employment.tooltipTargetType: Choose whether this employee's performance is measured by number of orders or total revenue generated
employment.tooltipTargetTypeOrdersSelected: Orders count based: performance is measured by how many orders the employee completes in the month. Targets and payroll deductions use the gap in order count versus the monthly target.
employment.tooltipTargetTypeRevenueSelected: Revenue based: performance is measured by total revenue (SAR) in the month. Targets and payroll deductions use the gap in revenue versus the monthly target.
employment.tooltipOrdersTarget: Monthly target number of orders this employee should complete
employment.tooltipRevenueTarget: Monthly target revenue amount (in SAR) this employee should generate
employment.tooltipTargetDeductionType: How deductions are calculated when target is not met. Fixed: flat amount per missing order/revenue. Tiers: deduction based on company-defined ranges.
employment.tooltipDeductionFixedSelected:
Fixed deduction:
A fixed amount (set company-wide) is deducted for each order below the monthly target.
employment.tooltipDeductionOrdersTiersSelected:
Order tiers:
The shortfall in order count is mapped to consecutive tiers; each tier applies a different deduction amount (defined company-wide).
employment.tooltipDeductionRevenueTiersSelected:
Revenue tiers:
Shortfall is split into 1 to 8 contiguous SAR bands. For each crossed portion: (allocated SAR ÷ unit amount) × that band's SAR-per-unit rate. Any extra shortfall repeats the strictest band's rate.
employment.tooltipSalaryAmount: Base monthly salary for this employee. Must meet company minimum salary requirement.
employment.tooltipNotes: Optional notes about this employee's operating conditions or special arrangements

documents.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this document?
documents.searchPlaceholder: Search by doc name or source

HR_RECRUITMENT_VISA_DEADLINE_SOON.name: Visa deadline soon
HR_RECRUITMENT_VISA_DEADLINE_SOON.longDescription: A recruitment candidate has a visa deadline approaching in {days} days. Please review and take action if needed.

HR_RECRUITMENT_ARRIVAL_SOON.name: Candidate arriving soon
HR_RECRUITMENT_ARRIVAL_SOON.longDescription: A recruitment candidate is expected to arrive within 5 days. Please prepare for onboarding if needed.

HR_EMPLOYMENT_IQAMA_EXPIRY_SOON.name: Iqama expiry soon
HR_EMPLOYMENT_IQAMA_EXPIRY_SOON.longDescription: An employment record has an iqama expiry date in {days} days. Please ensure renewal or follow-up.

HR_ASSETS_PENDING_RECOVERY.name: Assets pending recovery
HR_ASSETS_PENDING_RECOVERY.shortDescription: Assets assigned to employee pending recovery
HR_ASSETS_PENDING_RECOVERY.longDescription: Assets have been assigned to an employee whose employment has ended. Recovery of these assets is pending.

HR_ASSETS_LOSS_REPORT_APPROVAL_REQUIRED.name: Loss report approval required
HR_ASSETS_LOSS_REPORT_APPROVAL_REQUIRED.shortDescription: Asset loss report requires your approval
HR_ASSETS_LOSS_REPORT_APPROVAL_REQUIRED.longDescription: An asset loss report has been submitted and requires approval before it can be processed.

HR_ASSETS_LOSS_REPORT_APPROVED.name: Loss report approved
HR_ASSETS_LOSS_REPORT_APPROVED.longDescription: An asset loss report has been approved and processed.

HR_ASSETS_LOSS_REPORT_REJECTED.name: Loss report rejected
HR_ASSETS_LOSS_REPORT_REJECTED.longDescription: An asset loss report has been rejected.

PAYROLL_MONTH_APPROVAL_DEADLINE_SOON.name: Payroll settings approval due
PAYROLL_MONTH_APPROVAL_DEADLINE_SOON.shortDescription: Payroll settings for this month are not approved ({days_remaining} days left)
PAYROLL_MONTH_APPROVAL_DEADLINE_SOON.longDescription: Fewer than 7 days remain in the current month (Saudi Arabia). Approve payroll settings for {payroll_year}-{payroll_month} so salary calculations can use the locked configuration.

common.name: Name

common.fullNameEnRequired: Full name in English is required

common.unsavedChangesDraft: You have unsaved changes. Save as draft?

common.employmentStatTipTotalEmployees: All employees in your company (all statuses). Click to clear the status filter from the table.
common.employmentStatTipOnDuty: Employees currently active and on duty. Click to filter the table by active status.
common.employmentStatTipOnboarding: Employees under procedure (in progress). Click to filter the table.
common.employmentStatTipDeserted: Employees marked as deserted. Click to filter the table.
common.employmentStatTipDeactivated: Employees who have been deactivated. Click to filter the table.
common.confirmMarkAsDeserted: Are you sure you want to mark {name} as Deserted? You can change the status later from the edit form.
common.confirmRestoreToInProgress: Are you sure you want to restore {name} to In Progress?
common.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this candidate?
common.confirmDeleteEmployee: Are you sure you want to delete this employee? This action cannot be undone.
common.unsavedPrompt: You have unsaved changes. Do you want to leave?
common.confirmActivate: Are you sure you want to activate this employee? This will change status to Active.
common.confirmDeactivate: Are you sure you want to deactivate this employee?
common.confirmReactivate: Are you sure you want to reactivate this employee?
common.cannotDeleteActiveEmployee: Active employees cannot be deleted. Deactivate the employee first if you need to remove them from active duty.

common.employmentWarning: Note: Employee status cannot be set to Active unless all mandatory documents are uploaded and valid.
common.responsibleOfficeNumberMaxDigits: Responsible office number must be at most 10 digits

dailyOps.searchByNameOrCode: Search by name (AR/EN) or employee code
dailyOps.dateLockedToToday: Date is locked to today (server time - Riyadh).
dailyOps.dateMaxToday: Date cannot be in the future.

dailyOps.differenceHint: Automatically calculated from received and collected cash.
dailyOps.differenceWarning: Difference is negative; please double check the amounts.
dailyOps.confirmDeleteDraft: Are you sure you want to delete this draft operation?
dailyOps.noLogs: No logs in this date range

fleet.title: Fleet Management
fleet.deleteOnlyWhenIdle: Vehicle can only be deleted when status is Idle. Unassign or exit maintenance first.
fleet.validationCostMustBePositive: Cost must be a positive number
fleet.validationOdometerMustBePositive: Odometer must be a positive number
fleet.validationOdometerMustBeAtLeast: Odometer must be at least {value} km (current reading)
fleet.searchVehicle: License plate, model, VIN, or driver
fleet.validationGasQuantityPositive: Gas quantity must be a positive number
fleet.validationGasCostPositive: Gas cost must be a positive number
fleet.vehicleInMaintenanceCannotAddGas: Vehicle in maintenance cannot be linked to a gas record

payrollConfig.tabDescPayroll: Configure payroll settings, deduction models, and bonuses for your company.
payrollConfig.tabDescCosts: Manage and track company operational costs.
payrollConfig.approveConfirmMessage: This will lock the settings for {month} and make them available for salary calculations. This action cannot be undone. Continue?
payrollConfig.infoLegalText: Any modification to this page affects new calculations according to system rules, and does not reverse what was previously calculated within the approved payrolls.
payrollConfig.warningAppliesNextMonth: Settings have been modified after approval. Changes will apply next month, not the current month.
payrollConfig.payrollApprovalDeadlineWarning: {days} days or fewer remain in the current month (Saudi Arabia). This month’s payroll settings are not approved yet—approve them so salary calculations can run.
payrollConfig.generalSettingsDesc: Core payroll configuration including minimum salary, tips, and bonuses.
payrollConfig.fixedDeductionDesc: Simple fixed deduction per missing order.
payrollConfig.ordersDeductionTiersDesc: Progressive deduction based on missing orders count.
payrollConfig.revenueDeductionTiersDesc: Sequential deduction on revenue shortfall: consume the shortfall from the highest SAR band downward; each portion uses (amount ÷ unit) × that band’s SAR-per-unit rate. Use 1 to 8 contiguous bands from the first missing riyal.
payrollConfig.saveConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to save these changes?
payrollConfig.tipRecipientRepresentativeDesc: Tips go directly to the delivery representative
payrollConfig.tipRecipientCompanyDesc: Tips are collected by the company
payrollConfig.countBonusAmount: Bonus amount for each additional order
payrollConfig.fixedDeductionHelp: This model is suitable for companies that want a simple and clear way to calculate deduction.
payrollConfig.ordersTiersDescription: Define up to nine contiguous bands of missing orders (from 1 upward, no gaps). Each tier applies its deduction rate (SAR per missing order) to the portion of shortfall inside that band.
payrollConfig.revenueTiersDescription: Define 1 to 8 contiguous bands of revenue shortfall (SAR, from 1 upward, no gaps or overlaps). Set the unit amount (e.g. 16 SAR) and, for each band, the SAR deducted per unit amount of shortfall allocated to that band. Shortfall beyond all bands uses the strictest band’s (lowest range) rate per unit.
payrollConfig.readOnlyPastMonth: This is a snapshot from {month}. Settings are read-only.
payrollConfig.needsApproval: Settings must be approved before use.
payrollConfig.methodOrders: Deduct based on the number of orders
payrollConfig.methodFixed: Fixed deduction for each incomplete order
payrollConfig.validationFromTo: From must be less than or equal to To
payrollConfig.validationStartAtOne: Set the first tier to start at 1.
payrollConfig.validationBoundIntegerPositive: Enter valid positive whole numbers in From and To.
payrollConfig.validationGapOrOverlap: Keep tiers continuous: each next From must equal previous To + 1 (no gaps or overlaps).
payrollConfig.validationOrdersCountRange: Use 3 to 9 rows for Orders tiers.
payrollConfig.validationRevenueCountRange: Use 1 to 8 rows for Revenue tiers.
payrollConfig.validationFixTitle: Please correct the tier structure before saving.
payrollConfig.validationTiersInvalid: Orders tiers must be 3-9 contiguous bands from 1. Revenue tiers must be 1-8 contiguous bands from 1. Revenue also needs a positive unit amount.
payrollConfig.saveDisabledFixValidation: Fix validation errors in this section before saving
payrollConfig.tooltipCompanyWideSettings: These settings apply to all employees across the company
payrollConfig.tooltipOrdersDeductionTiers: Deduction amounts based on how many orders an employee is short of their target. Applies to employees using Orders Tiers deduction type.
payrollConfig.tooltipRevenueDeductionTiers: Revenue shortfall is applied sequentially from the highest band downward; each portion uses your SAR-per-unit rate (allocated amount ÷ unit amount). 1 to 8 contiguous bands. Applies to employees using Revenue Tiers deduction type.
payrollConfig.badgeIncompleteHelp: This section is not fully configured. Complete all required fields so payroll can be approved.
payrollConfig.helpMinimumSalary: Minimum gross salary used as a floor when adding new employees on the Employment page.
payrollConfig.helpTipRecipient: Controls whether daily tips are attributed to the representative or to the company for payroll purposes.
payrollConfig.helpCountBonus: When enabled, adds a fixed bonus amount for each order above the employee’s monthly target.
payrollConfig.helpRevenueBonus: When enabled, adds a bonus when revenue exceeds the employee’s monthly revenue target.
payrollConfig.calcExampleFixed: If a representative is {missing} orders below target and the rate is {rate} SAR per missing order: deduction = {missing} × {rate} = {total} SAR.
payrollConfig.calcExampleOrders: If a representative is {deficit} orders below target with your tier rates, progressive deduction totals {total} SAR.
payrollConfig.calcExampleRevenue: If revenue is {deficit} SAR below target and the unit is {unit} SAR, progressive per-unit deductions total {total} SAR.
payrollConfig.calcBreakdownIntroRevenue: Example with {deficit} SAR revenue shortfall and unit {unit} SAR (bands applied from highest downward):
payrollConfig.calcRevenueProgressiveBeyond: Beyond all bands (strictest rate): applicable {applicable} {curr} → {units} units (÷{unit} {curr}) × {rate} {curr}/unit = {subtotal} {curr}

salariesPayroll.title: Salaries Payroll
salariesPayroll.paymentDateMonthOnlyHint: Only dates in payroll month {month} are allowed.
salariesPayroll.belowAverageCostHint: Total revenue is less than average cost for this month.

statsTip.activeEmployees: Count of employees included in this payroll run.
statsTip.totalRevenue: Sum of total revenue from daily operations for all employees in this month.
statsTip.totalSalariesDue: Sum of net salaries still unpaid for this month.
statsTip.totalDeductions: Sum of performance deductions, daily-operation deductions, and scheduled loan installments.
statsTip.totalUncollectedCash: Sum of unreceived cash (cash collected minus cash received in daily operations).

salariesPayroll.needsApprovalBody: Payroll for {month} is not approved yet. Approve payroll settings to generate salaries from daily operations.
salariesPayroll.deductionHelp_DEDUCTION_FIXED: A fixed SAR amount is deducted for each order below the monthly target.
salariesPayroll.deductionHelp_DEDUCTION_ORDERS_TIERS: Order shortfall is split into tiers; each tier applies its deduction rate to the portion inside that tier.
salariesPayroll.deductionHelp_DEDUCTION_REVENUE_TIERS: Revenue shortfall is split into SAR bands; each portion is (shortfall in band ÷ unit) × the tier’s SAR per unit.
salariesPayroll.targetDiffHint: Difference versus monthly target (orders or SAR depending on target type).
salariesPayroll.totalDeductionsHint: Performance deduction plus daily-operation deductions plus scheduled loan installments.
salariesPayroll.tipsBonusHint: Tips from daily operations plus eligible config bonuses.
salariesPayroll.finalSalaryHint: Net pay after deductions, bonuses, carryover, and operations deductions.
salariesPayroll.targetDeductionExplain: This is the tier/fixed performance deduction only (before operations deductions and loans).
salariesPayroll.selectDifferenceProcessing: Select how to handle the difference
salariesPayroll.diff_deferral: Deferral to next month (creates a carryover line)
salariesPayroll.differenceManualPlaceholder: Describe how the difference was settled
salariesPayroll.cashDifferenceDeducted: A negative difference is treated as a deduction from this month's salary.
salariesPayroll.breakdownTargetMetNoShortfall: The monthly performance target was met or exceeded. No performance shortfall deduction applies.
salariesPayroll.breakdownNoDetailShortfall: A shortfall was calculated, but no step-by-step tier breakdown was stored. Regenerating payroll after configuration changes may add detail.
salariesPayroll.breakdownRevenueFixedNote: Revenue-based targets with “fixed per order” deduction do not apply a performance deduction in this configuration.

help.employeeCard: Employee identity, payroll month, payment status, and operating platform for this payroll run.
help.workingDays: Number of calendar days with recorded daily operations in this month.
help.ordersRevenue: Orders or total revenue from approved daily operations for this month, depending on target type.
help.targetDiff: Gap between achieved performance and the monthly target (orders or SAR). Negative means below target.
help.performanceDeduction: Performance-only deduction from company rules (fixed per missing order, order tiers, or revenue bands). Does not include loan installments or daily-operation line deductions.
help.tableLoans: Scheduled asset/loan installments deducted in this payroll month.
help.tableCash: Cash collected vs received from daily operations; difference reflects uncollected cash affecting payroll.
help.tableOtherDeductions: Other deduction amounts recorded on daily operations in this month.
help.tableTipsBonuses: Tips from daily operations plus any eligible configuration bonuses.
help.finalSalary: Net amount due for this month after performance rules, operations deductions, loans, tips/bonuses, and carryover.
help.operatingCard1: Contractual base salary and how the monthly performance target is defined.
help.operatingCard2: Target value and the deduction method used when the target is not met.
help.operatingCard3: Delivery platform and the employee’s platform user identifier.
help.tabSalary: Operational results and payroll calculation for the selected month.
help.tabOperating: Employment operating parameters used for targets and deductions.
